bull.b: Sustained commercial momentum (61% YoY sales growth, record patient starts and +21% account penetration) indicates durable adoption in oncology practice. Management's multi-year ramp targets imply predictable scaling potential, supporting durable revenue expansion if reimbursement and uptake continue.

Material balance-sheet improvement with minimal debt and positive equity reduces refinancing and solvency risk, giving management strategic flexibility. A conservative leverage profile supports funding commercialization and clinical milestones without immediate high-interest obligations, improving operational stability

A $54M capital raise plus $167M in liquidity and sustained expense cuts extend runway through key 2026–2027 readouts. Demonstrated spending discipline preserves cash for prioritized programs and commercialization, reducing near-term financing needs and enabling focused execution of clinical/commercial plans.

bear.b: Large negative operating and free cash flows indicate the company cannot self‑fund growth from operations and remains dependent on external financing. Persistent cash burn raises dilution and refinancing risk, constraining long‑term investment in commercialization and multiple clinical programs without successful reado

Extremely negative operating and net margins versus modest revenue demonstrate an unsustainable cost-to-revenue structure. Even with revenue rebound, earnings volatility and large losses undermine the path to profitability absent meaningful revenue scale or structural cost reductions, limiting financial resilience.

bear.t: Clinical And Regulatory Binary Risk For Tagmokitug (CCR8 Class)
bear.b: Mixed external CCR8 outcomes and immature internal data create a high binary risk for tagmokitug's clinical and regulatory trajectory. Key durability and survival endpoints need longer follow-up; unfavorable readouts or class skepticism could materially impair pipeline value and future oncology revenue prospects.
